title: Pseudognaphalium antennarioides
intro: Pseudognaphalium antennarioides, also known as Antennaria-like cudweed, is a perennial plant species in the Asteraceae family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The health benefits of Pseudognaphalium antennarioides include its potential to treat inflammatory conditions, skin issues, and respiratory problems due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Pseudognaphalium antennarioides is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is traditionally used in folk medicine to soothe skin irritations and calm respiratory issues.
